// sections/02-structure.adoc

== Structure of the Registry and Registers

=== Registry Architecture
The S-100 Geospatial Information (GI) Registry is hosted on an infrastructure managed by the IHO Secretariat. It functions as a centralized information system containing a collection of independent but interconnected Registers.

=== Types of Registers
The Registry comprises distinct types of Registers to support the development and implementation of S-100 based products:

==== Feature Concept Register
Contains definitions and descriptions of the feature types, feature attributes, and enumerated values used to build Feature Catalogues for data products.

==== Portrayal Register
Contains a collection of defined portrayals, symbols, and viewing rules mapped to the Feature Catalogues to support human-readable presentations of data.

==== Metadata Register
Maintains definitions and schemas for discovery metadata and quality indicators associated with geographic datasets.

==== Product Specifications Register
A list of S-100 based Product Specifications created by recognized organizations describing meta-information about the content, purpose, version, and availability of those specifications.

==== Data Producer Code Register
Contains unique identification codes assigned to organizations authorized to produce hydrographic data products and official Electronic Navigational Charts (ENCs).

=== Domains and Control Bodies
Within specific registers (such as Feature Concept, Portrayal, and Metadata), entries are classified into recognized **Domains** (e.g., Nautical Charts, Nautical Publications, Inland ENC).

* **Domain Control Body (DCB):** Each domain is overseen by a DCB consisting of technical experts nominated by Member States and international organizations. The DCB is responsible for evaluating proposals submitted to their respective domain.
* **Executive Control Body (ECB):** Consists of representatives from each domain to monitor registry operations, advise the Register Manager, and arbitrate in the event of disputes or technical appeals.

=== Stakeholder Roles and Responsibilities

==== Register Owner
The International Hydrographic Organization (IHO) serves as the Register Owner, establishing the registry and defining its overarching governance and policies.

==== Register Manager
The organization delegated by the Owner to maintain the Registry platform. The IHO Secretariat (TSSO) acts as the Register Manager, executing approved changes and managing the system's technical infrastructure.

==== Submitting Organizations
Organizations authorized by the Register Owner to submit proposals for new items, modifications, or retirement of existing items in the registers.
